title: Micron Signs Up 16 "Strategic Customer Agreements" For Memory In A 5-Year Plan That Cannot Be Cancelled After Earning As Much Revenue As Much of The Company's Entire History
description: Micron says that it has locked in 16 memory customers in an unbreakable agreement, hinting that high prices will continue for several years.

title: Memory Shortages Have Destroyed The Consumer Segment As DRAM Prices Surge By Up To 89% In Q2 2026

description: Samsung & SK Hynix have announced a multi-billion-dollar expansion plan for AI and chip manufacturing in Korea.

title: Micron to Pour $250 Billion in Manufacturing on US Soil As It Starts Construction of New York's DRAM Megafab
description: Micron has announced that it will invest $250 billion in America as it lays down the first concrete for the New York DRAM Megafab.

title: South Korea's SK Hynix Launching $28 Billion US Listing To Ride Global AI Wave - Slashdot
description: SK Hynix is launching a Nasdaq listing expected to raise about $28 billion, giving US investors easier access to one of the biggest beneficiaries of the AI memory-chip boom. Reuter

title: id Software Issues Statement Following Xbox Layoffs, Addresses Concern Over Future
description: id Software has issued a statement following layoffs at Xbox that saw roughly half the staff at the legendary first-person shooter studio lose their jobs.

title: Alarm over launch of facial recognition in UK shops that instantly alerts police  | Facial recognition | The Guardian
description: Civil liberties groups say Facewatch system in stores such as Sainsbury’s and B&M is ‘dangerous escalation’

title: Guilty until proven innocent: shoppers falsely identified by facial recognition system struggle to clear their names | Facial recognition | The Guardian

title: Cuba hit by another blackout, the second this week | AP News

description: DuckDuckGo Browser now blocks most YouTube video ads by default on several platforms. It’s a feature many users will celebrate, but I can’t help feeling a bit conflicted about it.
